metadata.title: When Does Debt Create Value? The MM Trade-Off
metadata.note: CLI video. Modigliani-Miller trade-off theory of capital structure.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

lines: In a perfect world, debt is irrelevant.
Add taxes: debt creates T_c * D in value.

beats.narration_text: In a perfect world with no taxes and no distress, debt is irrelevant. Add one friction: corporate taxes. Debt creates value equal to T_c times D. Add distress costs and you get a hump: the optimal capital structure.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- MM I: V is constant. MM with tax: V = V_U + T_c*D. Trade-off: peak at D*.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: In a perfect world with no taxes and no distress, debt is irrelevant. Add one friction: corporate taxes. Debt creates value equal to T_c times D. Add distress c

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to compute three firm value curves: no taxes, with tax shield, and with trade-off theory including distress costs.

props.command: claude "Compute firm value curves: V_U=1000, T_c=0.25, distress=0.0003*D^2. For D in [0,100,...,800]: V_no_tax=1000, V_tax=V_U+T_c*D, V_tradeoff=V_U+T_c*D-distress. Find optimal D."

beats.narration_text: The script computes three firm value curves across a debt range and finds the optimal D by maximizing V in the trade-off model.

props.code:
V_U=1000; T_c=0.25
Ds=list(range(0,850,100))
for D in Ds:
    distress=0.0003*D**2
    V_no=V_U
    V_tax=V_U+T_c*D
    V_to=V_U+T_c*D-distress
    print(f"D={D}: "
          f"no_tax={V_no} "
          f"with_tax={V_tax:.0f} "
          f"tradeoff={V_to:.0f}")
D_opt=T_c/(2*0.0003)
print(f"Optimal D: {D_opt:.0f}")

beats.narration_text: Three curves appear. The flat line: no-tax MM. The rising line: tax shield benefit. The humped trade-off curve: optimal D-to-V of 38 percent where the tax shield just offsets distress costs.
beats.visual_intent: Manim B04_MMTaxShield: three curves on V vs D axes with optimal D marker.

beats.narration_text: The hump tells you where to stop. Beyond D equals 417 million, each dollar of new debt destroys more in distress costs than it gains in tax shield.

props.command: claude "Now vary T_c: compute optimal D for T_c in [0.15, 0.21, 0.25, 0.30]. Show how optimal leverage rises with the corporate tax rate."

beats.narration_text: Higher tax rates justify more debt. The trade-off theory predicts that high-tax firms should carry more leverage. This is a testable prediction. It has empirical support.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- optimal D rises with T_c. High-tax firms: more leverage justified. Testable prediction.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Higher tax rates justify more debt. The trade-off theory predicts that high-tax firms should carry more leverage. This is a testable prediction. It has empirica

beats.narration_text: The teardown: debt is not good or bad. It is good up to the hump. The hump location depends on your tax rate and your distress cost structure.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- debt is good up to the hump. The hump location is your optimal D.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The teardown: debt is not good or bad. It is good up to the hump. The hump location depends on your tax rate and your distress cost structure.

beats.narration_text: Next: calibrate distress cost function from credit spreads. Follow for more corporate finance teardowns.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next steps: calibrate distress cost from credit spread data.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Next: calibrate distress cost function from credit spreads. Follow for more corporate finance teardowns.

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
